Name: WOMB (ウーム)
Known For: Iconic 4-floor Shibuya super-club, world-class DJs, massive main-floor LED ceiling
Reservations: No reservations needed; arrive before midnight to avoid longest queues
Notes: DJ Mag Top 100. 4 floors with LED infinity ceiling. House/techno/EDM depending on night. Reddit: THE club for electronic music. Open Fri-Sat 11pm-5am+. Bring passport for ID. All-male groups face extra scrutiny. Multiple sources: Reddit r/JapanTravel, DJ Mag, TripAdvisor.

Name: Sound Museum Vision (VISION)
Known For: Premier Shibuya club, 4 rooms with different genres
Reservations: No reservation needed; VIP tables available for groups
Notes: 4 distinct rooms for different music tastes. Very foreigner-friendly. Reddit: more accessible than WOMB for mixed-taste groups. Open Fri-Sat 11pm-5am. Multiple sources: Reddit r/JapanTravel, r/Tokyo, TripAdvisor.

Name: ageHa (アゲハ)
Known For: Japan's largest nightclub, legendary venue for massive EDM/trance events
Reservations: No reservations; buy advance tickets online
Notes: NOTE: Original closed Jan 2022, check current status. When operational: pool area, outdoor garden, 3 dance floors, capacity 2,400+. Free shuttle from Shibuya. Reddit: nothing else in Tokyo matches the scale. Multiple sources: Reddit, DJ Mag, Resident Advisor.

Name: 1OAK Tokyo (ワンオーク東京)
Known For: NYC-imported luxury nightclub, celebrity sightings, bottle service, hip-hop/Top 40
Price: Cover 3,000-5,000 yen men; bottles from 50,000 yen
Notes: Roppongi upscale club. Hip-hop/R&B/Top 40. Dress code enforced. Bottle service starts 50,000 yen with guaranteed table. Ignore street touts. Open Fri-Sat 10pm-5am. Multiple sources: Reddit r/Tokyo, TripAdvisor.

Name: V2 Tokyo (ブイツー東京)
Known For: Massive Roppongi super-club, multiple floors, hip-hop/EDM mix
Price: Cover 2,000-4,000 yen (incl 1-2 drinks); bottles from 30,000 yen
Reservations: VIP tables bookable, recommended for bachelor parties
Notes: Very foreigner-friendly. Reddit: easiest big club for foreign groups. Great light/laser shows. Welcoming to bachelor parties -- ask about group packages. Gets crowded after 1am Saturdays. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, Google Reviews.

Name: Contact (コンタクト)
Known For: Underground techno/house club, audiophile-grade Funktion-One sound
Notes: Smaller underground alternative. Reddit: where the real techno heads go. No photos on dance floor. Intimate dark atmosphere. Multiple sources: Reddit, Resident Advisor.

Name: Robot Restaurant / Robot Show
Known For: Insane neon robot/dancer show, total sensory overload, THE tourist spectacle
Reservations: Yes - book in advance, group discounts may apply
Notes: NOTE: Verify current status for April 2025. Multiple shows nightly. Reddit: extremely touristy but worth doing once with a bachelor party. Food is mediocre. Arrive 30min early for lobby bar. Multiple sources: Reddit (thousands of mentions), TripAdvisor, Lonely Planet.

Name: Kagaya (かがや)
Known For: Legendary one-man-show izakaya, eccentric host Mark performs comedy skits
Reservations: Yes - MANDATORY. Call to reserve (Japanese only, have hotel call). Only ~12 seats
Notes: Reddit LEGEND. TripAdvisor 4.5/5, 700+ reviews. PERFECT for bachelor parties -- Mark loves groups. Reservations essential, booked weeks out. Food is decent izakaya fare. Hours ~5pm-11pm, closed Sundays. Multiple sources: Reddit (one of most recommended places), TripAdvisor, Atlas Obscura.

Name: Samurai Rock (サムライロック)
Known For: Samurai-themed cocktail bar, bartender in full samurai armor
Reservations: Not required but recommended for groups of 5+
Notes: Great pre-club Roppongi stop. Bartender performs sword demonstrations. Great photo ops for bachelor party. Open ~6pm-2am. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, Google Reviews.

Name: Golden Gai (ゴールデン街)
Known For: Historic 6-alley maze of 200+ tiny bars (5-10 seats each)
Price: 500-1,500 yen cover per bar + 700-1,500 yen per drink
Reservations: No reservations; walk-in if space available
Notes: THE must-do drinking experience. Split bachelor party into pairs -- bars seat 5-8 only. Cover charge is normal. Best bars: Albatross, Open Book, Death Match in Hell. Peak 9pm-midnight. Go weekday if possible. Multiple sources: Reddit (hundreds of threads), TimeOut, Lonely Planet.

Name: Omoide Yokocho / Memory Lane (思い出横丁)
Known For: Post-war alley of tiny yakitori stalls, smoky nostalgic vibes
Notes: Also called Piss Alley. Feels like 1950s Tokyo. Yakitori skewers 100-300 yen, beer 500 yen. Split your group. Best stalls: Asadachi, Wakare. Open 5pm-midnight+. Don't wear nice clothes. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, Lonely Planet.

Name: Vampire Cafe (ヴァンパイアカフェ)
Known For: Gothic vampire-themed restaurant with coffin tables, blood-red decor
Notes: Full gothic vampire interior. Food is Italian/French and decent. TripAdvisor 4.0/5. Courses 3,000-5,000 yen. Open 5pm-11:30pm. Multiple sources: TripAdvisor, Reddit, Atlas Obscura.

Name: Albatross Bar (アルバトロス)
Known For: Iconic 3-story Golden Gai bar with chandeliers, red velvet, rooftop terrace
Notes: Most recommended Golden Gai bar on Reddit. 3 floors in a building barely wider than a hallway. Tourist-friendly, English-speaking staff. Can fit ~15 people across all floors. Open 6pm-midnight+. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, TimeOut.

Name: Karaoke Kan (カラオケ館) Shibuya
Known For: Lost in Translation filming location (Room 601), massive song library
Reservations: Not required; recommended for 8+ on weekends
Notes: Can request Room 601 from the movie. Private rooms for 2-20+. Get nomihoudai (all-you-can-drink) plan. Cheapest before 7pm and weekdays. Free-time midnight-5am plans ~1,500-2,000/person. Bring conbini snacks. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor.

Name: Big Echo (ビッグエコー) Shinjuku
Known For: Largest karaoke chain in Japan, DAM scoring system, reliable quality
Reservations: Not required; walk-in usually fine except Fri/Sat after 10pm
Notes: 500+ locations. DAM scoring = great for bachelor party competitions. English interface available. Get 2hr nomihoudai minimum. Use Daiichikosho app for English songs. Open noon-5am. Multiple sources: Reddit, Google Reviews.

Name: Uta Hiroba (歌広場)
Known For: Budget king of karaoke, cheapest rates in Tokyo
Notes: Budget option -- half the price of Big Echo/Karaoke Kan. Rooms are basic but functional. Same JOYSOUND/DAM machines. Free-time midnight-5am plans absurdly cheap. Multiple locations in Kabukicho. Open noon-6am. Multiple sources: Reddit, budget travel blogs.

Name: JOYSOUND (ジョイサウンド) Shibuya
Known For: Premium chain with largest song catalog in Japan (300,000+ songs)
Price: 500-2,000 yen/hr; VIP and party plans available
Reservations: Recommended for VIP/party rooms on weekends
Notes: Best English song library in Japan per Reddit. VIP concept rooms with stage lighting and disco balls. Party room plans for 10-20 people include room+drinks+food. Open noon-5am. Multiple sources: Reddit, JOYSOUND official.

Name: Pasela (パセラ) Shinjuku
Known For: Upscale karaoke with resort-style themed rooms, famous honey toast
Reservations: Recommended for party plans and large groups
Notes: THE premium karaoke option for bachelor parties. Themed rooms (Bali, VIP suites). Famous honey toast. Craft cocktails. Party plans include room+drinks+food course. VIP rooms fit 20+ people. Open 11am-5am. Multiple sources: Reddit, TripAdvisor, TimeOut.
